offers.title: £50 gift card when you choose an EDF Simply Fixed or Simply Tracker dual fuel tariff
offers.description:
From 16 February to 6 April 2026, each friend you successfully refer to EDF will receive £75 and you’ll be rewarded with £100.↵ And the best part? There’s no limit to how many friends you can share the offer with… or how much you can earn.↵ Sign up as a new customer or, if you’re already with EDF, simply switch to a fixed tariff. If you choose a dual fuel fixed tariff, you’ll get a £50 gift card.
Rated Excellent on Trustpilot.↵ Here’s what you’ll get:↵ A £50 gift card when you purchase a dual fuel tariff (both gas and electricity)↵ This must be either the simply fixed or simply tracker tariff to be eligible for the gift card.↵ Free electric on Christmas day - see terms here https://www.edfenergy.com/free-elec/december-terms-and-conditions?lightbox=true↵ Choose your gift card from Amazon.co.uk, Merlin Days Out, Aldi, Tesco, ASOS or The Restaurant Group.↵ How to get your gift card:↵ • You’ll only get your gift card if you complete your purchase using the link below.↵ • Simply select ‘I’m not with EDF’ or ‘I’m with EDF’ when directed to EDF’s website to continue as a new or existing customer.↵ • The gift card cannot be claimed if the contract with EDF is cancelled within 3 months of going live.↵ • Please provide the email address you would like the gift card to be sent to when prompted, prior to purchasing your tariff. You will receive an email from the gift card provider when you do this, please note this is not your final gift card.↵ • You’ll pick the gift card you’d like when instructions are sent 120 days after your tariff start date from no-reply@giftcloud.com. EDF will provide your details securely to the third-party gift card supplier (Gift Cloud).↵ • Gift Cloud will then send you instructions on how you claim your gift card within 30 days, and you’ll need to claim it within 90 days.↵ • The Gift Card redemption process can take up to a total of 120 days to arrive after your contract start date, if you have not received the gift card by this time please contact voucherqueries@edfenergy.com.↵ ↵ From 1 October to 31 December 2025 the energy price cap will be £1,755 a year. Our Simply Fixed Sep26 tariff has a typical bill price of £1,598 a year – £157 cheaper than Ofgem's price cap from 1 October. Exit fees apply.↵ Figures are based on Ofgem ‘typical’ consumption. 'Typical' yearly consumption is 2,700kWh electricity and 11,500kWh gas based on a national average of regional prices for a dual fuel customer with a standard electricity meter who pays by Direct Debit. 'Typical' consumption assumes the price cap does not change over a full year. In reality, the price cap changes every three months. Fixed tariff rates remain the same for the duration of the tariff and are not covered by the energy price cap.↵ The energy regulator Ofgem reviews and sets the maximum rates we can charge for gas and electricity on our variable tariffs. This is the energy price cap. The cap is reviewed every three months in January, April, July and October.↵ Please remember – it’s the standing charge and cost per unit of energy that’s a set rate – not your bill which will vary depending on how much energy you use.↵ See EDF's prediction for the price cap. It's updated weekly and can help you decide what's best for you. We use Ofgem's price cap methodology to predict future price cap levels. Our prediction could rise or fall, based mostly on changes in the wholesale cost of energy for future periods.

offers.description: Black Country Living Museum is an award-winning open-air museum that tells the story of one of the very first industrialised landscapes in Britain↵ Explore over 250 years of history, from the Industrial Revolution to post-war prosperity, through living interpretation.↵ Leave the 21st century behind for the day and experience sights, sounds, smells, and tastes of the Black Country as you explore over 80 rebuilt and recreated shops, houses and industrial workshops. In addition, you’ll meet our historic characters, who will share what it was really like to live and work during this revolutionary period of history.↵ Get ready for endless adventures! Every day ticket includes an UnChained Annual Pass with 12 months of free return visits. And with something new to see on every visit, you’ll want to come back again and again. For fans of screen tourism, the Museum was used for several filming locations in the global smash-hit drama Peaky Blinders. Explore authentic screen-used sets and immerse yourself in the atmosphere of Charlie's Yard. For Peaky Blinders fans, this is an experience not to be missed.
